task_completion.description: Verify the promotion name is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Summer Sale 20% Off' entered into the 'Name' input field on the Add/Edit Promotion page in Drupal Commerce.

task_completion.description: Verify the offer type is set to a percentage discount on the order subtotal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Percentage off the order subtotal' (or similar percentage-based order discount) selected in the 'Offer type' dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: Verify the discount amount is set to 20%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the number '20' entered into the percentage or amount input field within the Offer configuration section.

task_completion.description: Verify the coupon code 'SUMMER20' is added to the promotion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'SUMMER20' entered into the 'Coupon code' input field or listed as an attached coupon in the Coupons section of the promotion form.

task_completion.description: Verify the promotion is saved and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a green success message stating the promotion 'Summer Sale 20% Off' has been created/saved, OR look for the promotion in the main Promotions list with an 'Active' status.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with the Drupal Commerce GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (e.g., Chrome, Firefox) should be visible displaying the Drupal admin interface, rather than terminal windows executing database commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Drupal application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show genuine Drupal UI elements, form fields, and dropdowns being manipulated, not a text editor or static mockups.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows open showing SQL commands (e.g., INSERT INTO commerce_promotion) or database management tools being used to complete the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected promotion name is exactly 'Summer Sale 20% Off'. The expected coupon code is exactly 'SUMMER20'. The discount type must be a percentage (specifically off the order subtotal) and the discount amount must be '20'. These values should be visible in the Drupal Commerce promotion creation form or the promotions list.
